title: I need to talk to Mark Blyth the economist! I HAVE THE ANSWER!

enrichment.summary: Kyle proposes his solution to what he sees as the root economic problem: excessive overhead and credentialism. He argues economists like Mark Blyth diagnose problems but miss the fix, which is to radically simplify by shutting down schools, redeploying educators as self-directed bike-riding teachers, converting school buildings into homeless shelters, and relaxing housing and licensing regulations to eliminate unnecessary bureaucratic friction.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: too much overhead and bureaucratic friction, not debt itself
wasteful and exclusionary; people should just be given jobs and trusted
should be shut down and replaced with self-directed learning and educational videos
should be relaxed so people can build simple, cheap homes themselves
too abstract and fail to identify the real problem
proves society can trust unqualified people with hard tasks when necessary — should apply this logic to the economy
